text.title: Freelancer Discovery Call Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding freelancer-discovery-call-template. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Call template MUST cover all 5 sections: context, pain, success criteria, budget signals, decision process. Skipping any section produces gaps in the proposal.
rule.rationale: Each section maps to a specific proposal field; missing the budget signal collapses the proposal to a price guess.

rule.statement: Template MUST include at least one budget-signal question (current spend, comparable spend, allocated budget). Verbal-only budget signals are forbidden as commitment.
rule.rationale: Without budget signal the proposal is priced from hope, not data.

rule.statement: Output MUST include a scorecard (5-axis, 1-5): fit, urgency, budget, decision power, vibes. Lead with score <12 is no-go.
rule.rationale: Calibrated scorecard outperforms gut on close-rate by ~30%.

rule.statement: Output MUST include a 1-page proposal seed (problem, approach, scope, price, timeline). Multi-page proposals at discovery stage are forbidden.
rule.rationale: One-pager closes faster; multi-page proposals get stuck in committee even at solo-prospect stage.

rule.statement: Operator word-share MUST be ≤30% measured by token count in the call transcript. Above that, the call became a pitch.
rule.rationale: Discovery converts when prospect talks; pitching during discovery kills signal and trust.
